headline: D.C. United land Ordaz from LAFC in $2.375M deal
dek: The El Salvador international joins the Black-and-Red as a U22 Initiative player after 98 appearances in LAFC colors.
body: D.C. United have acquired forward Nathan Ordaz from LAFC for $2.375 million, with up to an additional $500,000 available in performance incentives. LAFC also retains a sell-on percentage on any future trade. Ordaz, 22, made 98 appearances for the Black and Gold across all competitions since signing as the club's fourth homegrown player in 2022, recording 14 goals and 12 assists. He carries 14 caps for El Salvador into his new posting in the nation's capital.

headline: Cuypers leads Musa and Messi by one at the top
dek: Chicago's Hugo Cuypers holds a slim advantage in the MLS scoring race with the season past its midpoint.
body: Hugo Cuypers of Chicago Fire FC sits first in the goals table on 13, one clear of Petar Musa (FC Dallas) and Leo Messi (Inter Miami CF), who are level on 12. Messi also ranks second in the assists race on seven, one behind LAFC's Son Heung-Min, who leads the league on eight. Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC shares second in assists with Messi on seven. Andrés Cubas of Vancouver Whitecaps tops the tackles leaderboard at 52, with Inter Miami's Yannick Bright second on 47 and D.C. United's João Peglow third on 45.

headline: Ingvartsen and Guilherme outrun the model by wide margins
dek: Two strikers are converting well beyond expectation, while Vancouver's Brian White carries a significant xG deficit.
body: San Diego FC's Marcus Ingvartsen leads MLS in xG outperformance with 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a gap of plus-4.3. Houston Dynamo's Guilherme is close behind at plus-4.2, having scored eight times from an xG of 3.8. On the other side, Vancouver's Brian White sits 2.4 goals below expectation, 10 goals scored against an xG of 12.4, the largest deficit in the league. Minnesota's Kelvin Yeboah is next at minus-1.8, having hit eight from an xG of 9.8.

headline: Nashville and San Jose sit atop their conferences
dek: Nashville's backline remains the stingiest in the league as San Jose pace the West on goal difference.
body: Nashville SC lead the Eastern Conference on 33 points, built on a 10W-3D-1L record. Their 11 goals allowed is the best defensive mark in MLS. San Jose Earthquakes top the West on 32 points at 10W-2D-3L, having scored 34 times in 15 outings. Inter Miami CF hold the league's most potent attack with 39 goals scored, seven more than any conference leader, though their table standing trails Nashville's in the East.

headline: Six players cross MLS borders in one window
dek: Gabriel Pirani heads to San Diego FC while two departures shrink the Galaxy and Whitecaps rosters.
body: Gabriel Pirani moves from D.C. United to San Diego FC. On the departure side, Gabriel Pec leaves LA Galaxy having logged 1,260 minutes this season, and Aziel Jackson exits Vancouver Whitecaps with 1,080 minutes on his ledger. Their destinations are unknown. Three arrivals offset the losses elsewhere: Yevhen Cheberko joins LAFC, Curtis Ofori signs with Red Bull New York, and Nate Edwards links up with Toronto FC.

headline: Cuypers leads at 13, Musa and Messi share second
dek: Chicago's striker holds a one-goal cushion over a Dallas forward and the Inter Miami talisman with the season still running.
body: Hugo Cuypers of Chicago Fire FC sits atop the scoring table with 13 goals. Petar Musa of FC Dallas and Leo Messi of Inter Miami CF are level at 12. Messi also ranks second in assists with seven, one behind LAFC's Son Heung-Min, who leads the league at eight. Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC shares second in assists with Messi at seven apiece.

headline: Ingvartsen beats his xG by 4.3 goals, the widest gap in MLS
dek: Two overperformers and two underperformers reveal a wide spread between expected and actual output across the league.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen of San Diego FC has scored 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a positive difference of 4.3. Guilherme of Houston Dynamo FC follows with eight goals against an xG of 3.8, a gap of plus-4.2. Going the other way, Brian White of Vancouver Whitecaps has scored 10 goals on an xG of 12.4, sitting 2.4 below expectation. Kelvin Yeboah of Minnesota United FC is 1.8 goals short of his xG of 9.8.

headline: Nashville and San Jose lead their conferences on 33 and 32 points
dek: Nashville's defense is the tightest in MLS at 11 goals allowed; Inter Miami's attack leads all clubs with 39 scored.
body: Nashville SC top the East with 33 points on a 10-3-1 record, having conceded just 11 goals across 14 matches, the best defensive return in the league. San Jose Earthquakes lead the West with 32 points at 10-2-3, scoring 34 goals while allowing 15. Inter Miami CF hold the league's most dangerous attack with 39 goals scored, regardless of conference standing. Andrés Cubas of Vancouver Whitecaps leads all players in tackles with 52.

headline: Rossi out at Columbus as three new faces arrive
dek: Diego Rossi departs the Crew while Austin FC, Columbus, and D.C. United each add a body.
body: Diego Rossi has left Columbus Crew after logging 1,290 minutes this season. His destination is unknown. Columbus moved quickly to offset the loss, bringing in Lautaro Giaccone. Austin FC have added Alonso Ramírez, while D.C. United have signed Fidel Barajas. The Crew's business is the most pointed of the three, coming on the same day their departed forward's season total was confirmed.

headline: Cuypers holds one-goal cushion in tight scoring race
dek: Chicago's Hugo Cuypers leads on 13 goals, with Petar Musa and Leo Messi one back at 12 apiece.
body: Hugo Cuypers of Chicago Fire FC sits atop the golden boot standings with 13 goals. FC Dallas striker Petar Musa and Inter Miami's Leo Messi are level in second at 12. Messi adds seven assists to his ledger, putting him in the thick of the creative race as well. Son Heung-Min of LAFC leads that category outright with eight, one clear of Messi and San Diego FC's Anders Dreyer.

headline: Ingvartsen and Guilherme beating the model by wide margins
dek: San Diego's Marcus Ingvartsen is outperforming his xG by 4.3 goals; Houston's Guilherme trails only slightly at plus-4.2.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen has scored 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a difference of plus-4.3, the largest overperformance in the league. Guilherme of Houston Dynamo FC is close behind at plus-4.2, with eight goals from 3.8 expected. At the other end, Vancouver's Brian White carries the league's largest deficit, sitting 2.4 goals below his xG of 12.4 despite already reaching double figures with ten.

headline: Nashville and San Jose lead their conferences by a point
dek: Nashville SC hold the East on 33 points; San Jose Earthquakes top the West on 32 with the sharper attack.
body: Nashville SC lead the Eastern Conference with 33 points from a 10-3-1 record and the stingiest defense in the league, having conceded just 11 goals. San Jose Earthquakes front the West on 32 points at 10-2-3, posting 34 goals scored. Inter Miami CF own the best attack in MLS with 39 goals for the season, though their defensive numbers have kept them off the top line in the East.
